comment: Curated BROAD example systems for the headless behavioral tier — the multi-aggregate systems that are NOT single-feature corpus fixtures. The per-feature cases come from the typed corpus manifest (test/fixtures/corpus/manifest.ts): run.mjs runs every feature declaring the `node` backend that carries a `test e2e`/`test` block, so there is no per-feature allowlist here. Each entry below is generated, its single `platform: node` (Hono) deployable booted on PGlite, and its emitted suites run. CONSTRAINT: exactly one node backend per system, so dispatch (host-agnostic, path-matched) is unambiguous — multi-backend systems (showcase, examples/acme) stay in the docker `conformance-full` leg. Tiers are DERIVED from the emitted file map, not the flags: `api`/`unit` here are only an inclusion hint (an entry with neither is UI-only → run-ui.mjs, skipped by run.mjs). `ui` runs the emitted `*.ui.spec.ts` Playwright round-trips against a built+served frontend (run-ui.mjs); `uiTier: "nightly"` = that UI round-trip runs only in the nightly frontend-fullstack matrix (run-ui.mjs --all), not the per-PR behavioral-ui gate.
